channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🌍 The Hormuz Hit to Helium | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Iran war is choking off a critical input for chipmaking and AI infrastructure.

item.title: 🏛️ From Slogan to Standard: How the Pentagon Should Define Affordable Mass | War on the Rocks
item.description: The term “affordable mass” entered public defense discourse in 2021 as a munitions concept, which the Air Force adopted in 2023 to describe its effort to field large numbers of lower-cost, semi-autonomous aircraft to complement crewed fighters. The term has since spread in…

item.title: 🌏 Beijing’s United Front and the Quiet Transfer of Western Technology | War on the Rocks
item.description: The military modernization and technological ambitions of the People’s Republic of China dominate headlines in Washington. Hypersonic missiles, AI breakthroughs, and an expanding navy are the visible symbols of competition with the Chinese Communist Party. But if the United…

item.title: 🌏 The Importance of the Battle of Savo Island | War on the Rocks
item.description: On the night of Aug. 9, 1942, an Allied fleet of 17 warships guarded the approaches to Guadalcanal. The fleet was newer, larger, and better equipped than the Japanese force bearing down on it. It had six heavy cruisers, two light cruisers, and nine destroyers. It carried radar,…

item.title: 🌍 The Iran War’s Threat to Turkey | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Even on the sidelines, Ankara faces blowback.

item.title: 📍 National Defence Strategy 2026: views in The Strategist | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The world is a more dangerous place than it was two years ago. The priorities outlined in Australia’s new National Defence Strategy (NDS) and its accompanying spending plan, the Integrated Investment Program (IIP), reflect this. ...

item.title: ⚛️ Rare Video Appears To Show A Soviet Su-7 Jet Dropping A Nuclear Bomb | The War Zone
item.description: During one very unusual Cold War drill, a Soviet Su-7 Fitter attack jet delivered a live tactical nuclear weapon in an end-to-end test. The post Rare Video Appears To Show A Soviet Su-7 Jet Dropping A Nuclear Bomb appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Carrier Tracker As of April 26, 2026 | The War Zone
item.description: The U.S. now has three carrier strike groups in the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility for the first time in more than two decades. The post Carrier Tracker As of April 26, 2026 appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Fool’s Gold: Speaker Johnson’s Section 702 proposal would place no limits on backdoor searches | Just Security
item.description: "Members can recognize the Johnson proposal for what it is: a transparent attempt to preserve the status quo rather than answer the bipartisan calls for needed reform." The post Fool’s Gold: Speaker Johnson’s Section 702 proposal would place no limits on backdoor searches…

item.title: 📍 The Poverty of the DOJ Indictment of the Southern Poverty Law Center | Just Security
item.description: Weissmann, former head of the DOJ Fraud Section, discusses conspicuous gaps in the indictment. The post The Poverty of the DOJ Indictment of the Southern Poverty Law Center appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌍 Digest of Recent Articles on Just Security (Apr. 19-24, 2026) | Just Security
item.description: U.S.-Israel-Iran War • Russia-Ukraine War • Reflections on International Law • FISA Section 702 • Immigration • Counterterrorism • Bosnia • Georgia • Tech Governance • U.S. Boat Strikes • Trump Executive Actions The post Digest of Recent Articles on Just Security (Apr. 19-24,…

item.title: 📍 On Anzac Day, reflect on wars of the past – and steel ourselves for wars of the future | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Anzac Day signifies tragedy. It speaks to futility and loss, the death of young Australians in their prime. We try to make sense of this ‘senseless loss’ by honouring their courage and extolling Anzac values. We ...

item.title: 🌍 U.S. Floats Punishing NATO Members for Refusing to Join Iran War | Foreign Policy
item.description: An internal Pentagon email suggests suspending Spain from the alliance and reviewing Britain’s claim to the Falkland Islands.

item.title: 📍 Space Force picks firms to develop Golden Dome’s space-based interceptors | Defense One
item.description: Defense giants and startups vie to create orbital defenses—even as the program’s czar concedes they may be unaffordable.

item.title: 🌏 What in the World? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Test yourself on the week of April 18: Taiwan’s president faces travel troubles, U.N. secretary-general candidates assemble in New York, and Pope Leo concludes his tour of Africa.

item.title: 📍 The Planet Is Doing Better Than You Think | Foreign Policy
item.description: Apocalyptic headlines overlook conservation and biodiversity successes.

item.title: 🔐 Satellites at the center: Inside the Pentagon’s next-gen space architecture | Breaking Defense
item.description: From emerging data networks to missile tracking and cyber resilience, Breaking Defense’s latest eBook brings together essential reporting on the evolving role of satellites in national security.

item.title: 📍 Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week | The War Zone
item.description: Another week! The post Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 U.S. Efforts To Prevent Iranian Mine Laying In Strait Of Hormuz Underway | The War Zone
item.description: Another U.S. delegation is headed to Pakistan to try to restart negotiations as a third carrier strike group arrives in the region. The post U.S. Efforts To Prevent Iranian Mine Laying In Strait Of Hormuz Underway appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Hoaxes Keep Escaping Containment | Foreign Policy
item.description: A delusional U.S. president is helping thin the line between fiction and reality.

item.title: 📍 How Gold and Regional Rivalries Drive the War in Sudan | Foreign Policy
item.description: The world’s worst humanitarian crisis enters its fourth year.

item.title: ⚛️ Space Force tasks a dozen companies for Golden Dome space-based interceptors | Breaking Defense
item.description: The service pledged it would demonstrate an “initial capability” for the orbital missile killers by 2028.

item.title: 🌏 The Staged Death of China’s Military-Civil Fusion | The Diplomat
item.description: Military-civil fusion became a major irritant in China-U.S. relations, so Beijing stopped talking about it. But it didn't stop pursuing the strategy.

item.title: 📍 Can Africa Follow Asia’s Development Model? | Foreign Policy
item.description: An economics writer known for his work on Asia turns his eye toward the fastest-growing continent.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine to field 25,000 ground robots in push to replace soldiers for frontline logistics | Defense News
item.description: Ground robots ran more than 9,000 frontline missions in March, triple November's count, and captured their first Russian position last summer.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine’s Drone-Hunting An-28 Turboprop Is Now Launching Interceptor Drones | The War Zone
item.description: The latest evolution of Ukraine’s rugged An-28 Cash turboprop counter-drone platform combines interceptor drones and Minigun armament. The post Ukraine’s Drone-Hunting An-28 Turboprop Is Now Launching Interceptor Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ US Air Force aims to double F-15EX Eagle fleet | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. Air Force plans to boost the number of F-15EX Eagle II aircraft in its inventory to 267.

item.title: 🔐 Decolonizing ‘Moby-Dick’ | Foreign Policy
item.description: Literature’s obsessive quest for anti-canonical adaptations pursues the white whale at its peril.

item.title: ⚛️ President Trump should secure America’s nuclear future by taking weapons out of DoE | Breaking Defense
item.description: America’s nuclear weapons arsenal is too central to national defense to be buried inside the Department of Energy, argues Franklin C. Miller and Frank A. Rose.

item.title: 📍 HH-60W Combat Rescue Helicopters To Take On Doomsday Evacuation Role In The Nation’s Capital | The War Zone
item.description: The Jolly Green IIs will be modified for VIP airlift and continuity of government ops, replacing aging UH-1N Twin Hueys in these roles. The post HH-60W Combat Rescue Helicopters To Take On Doomsday Evacuation Role In The Nation’s Capital appeared first on The War Zone.
